name: Naturepedia Soil Systems Map
description: Paid compressed relationship map connecting soil formation, soil horizons, soil microbiome, mycorrhizal networks, living soil food webs, nutrient cycling, soil carbon, soil biodiversity, soil water systems, regenerative soil systems, plants, microbes, carbon, and water.
architecture: Soil Systems → Soil Formation → Soil Horizons → Soil Microbiome → Mycorrhizal Networks → Soil Food Web → Nutrient Cycling → Soil Carbon → Soil Water → Regenerative Soil Systems

systemNodes.name: Soil Formation
systemNodes.role: Weathering, organic matter, mineral development, and landscape formation layer

systemNodes.name: Soil Horizons
systemNodes.role: Vertical soil profile and identification layer

systemNodes.name: Soil Microbiome
systemNodes.role: Microbial life, decomposition, nutrient transformation, and soil intelligence layer

systemNodes.name: Mycorrhizal Networks
systemNodes.role: Fungal-root exchange, resource sharing, and belowground communication layer

systemNodes.name: Living Soil Food Web
systemNodes.role: Organism interaction layer linking microbes, fungi, roots, insects, and decomposers

systemNodes.name: Nutrient Cycling
systemNodes.role: Transformation and movement of nutrients through living soil

systemNodes.name: Soil Carbon
systemNodes.role: Carbon storage, stabilization, decomposition, roots, fungi, and organic matter layer

systemNodes.name: Soil Water Systems
systemNodes.role: Infiltration, retention, groundwater recharge, drought buffering, and plant-available moisture layer

systemNodes.name: Regenerative Soil Systems
systemNodes.role: Applied restoration, farming, biodiversity, and resilience layer
